Introduction to Modern Chinese Literature and Culture

notes: COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. For more information see the New Policies for Transfer Student Applicants website for L&S: https://ls.berkeley.edu/prospective-students/new-policies-transfer-student-applicants By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete : 1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST): · Two transferable courses in English composition; · One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning; · Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas: o arts and humanities o social and behavioral sciences o physical and biological sciences AND 2) Major Requirements : Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. For more information on admission to UC Berkeley : http://admissions.berkeley.edu For specific information on transfer admission to UC Berkeley: https://admissions.berkeley.edu/apply-to-berkeley/transfer-students/transfer-requirements/ For more information on majors at UC Berkeley : Berkeley Academic Guide: http://guide.berkeley.edu

East Asian Humanities Major Articulation Information The East Asian Humanities major is a comparative study of East Asian cultures. The curriculum seeks to prepare students for cross-regional comparative work, emphasizing cultural and literary analysis, media studies, and interdisciplinary humanities. The major offers a high degree of flexibility, allowing students to draw coursework from two or more national traditions in East Asia (China, Japan, and South Korea, among others) and covering both the modern and classical periods. Students must complete 8 courses (or 40 units), and while we recommend acquiring a reading knowledge of at least one foreign language, the major can be entirely completed through English-taught courses. Coursework for requirements can be completed upon transfer to UC Berkeley. Students intending the major should enroll in at least one of the following classes in their first semester, along with upper-division courses from the list provided on our website or the Berkeley Guide . BUDDSTD/EALANG C 50 Introduction to Buddhism Chinese 7A Introduction to Modern Chinese Literature and Culture Chinese 7B Introduction to Pre-modern Chinese Literature and Culture Japanese 7A Introduction to Modern Japanese Literature and Culture Japanese 7B Introduction to Modern Pre-modern Literature and Culture Korean 7A Introduction to Modern Korean Literature and Culture Korean 7B Introduction to Pre-modern Chinese Literature and Culture Advising: Students with questions about transferring into the major in East Asian Humanities are encouraged to contact our undergraduate major advisor(UMA): Cassandra Dunn Department Website: http://ealc.berkeley.edu/ Email: cassandrajj@berkeley.edu Appointments: Schedule an appointment (in-person or remotely ).
